perturbation.original_text: Section 2.03 Payment of Purchase Price. On or before the Lease Commencement Date for any Schedule, the Company shall present to Lessor documentation ("Purchase Documentation"), in form and substance satisfactory to Lessor in its sole discretion, which (i) describes all units of Equipment ordered, received and accepted by the Company as agent for Lessor in connection with such Schedule, and (ii) if Company has paid any Supplier for any of the Equipment, includes evidence of the Purchase Price paid to Supplier for each such unit of Equipment and of passage of title thereto to Lessor. Upon the latter of (A) Lessor's receipt of the Purchase Documentation or (B) the satisfaction of all conditions precedent on or after the applicable Lease Commencement Date, Lessor shall pay the Supplier or reimburse the Company, as the case may be, for the aggregate Purchase Price for all Equipment purchased hereunder in connection with such schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.03 Payment of Purchase Price. On or before the Lease Commencement Date for any Schedule, the Company shall present to Lessor documentation ("Purchase Documentation"), in form and substance satisfactory to Lessor in its sole discretion, which (i) describes all units of Equipment ordered, received and accepted by the Company as agent for Lessor in connection with such Schedule, and (ii) if Company has paid any Supplier for any of the Equipment, includes evidence of the Purchase Price paid to Supplier for each such unit of Equipment and of passage of title thereto to Lessor. Upon the latter of (A) Lessor's receipt of the Purchase Documentation or (B) the satisfaction of all conditions precedent within 60 days of the applicable Lease Commencement Date, Lessor shall pay the Supplier or reimburse the Company, as the case may be, for the aggregate Purchase Price for all Equipment purchased hereunder in connection with such schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a 60-day limit to when the lessor should pay the purchase price. The original text states that payment should be done 'on or after the applicable Lease Commencement Date' but doesn't limit on when, while the changed text introduces a limit. The uncertainty arises as to which term takes precedence, creating an environment for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.03 Payment of Purchase Price. On or before the Lease Commencement Date for any Schedule, the Company shall present to Lessor documentation ("Purchase Documentation"), in form and substance satisfactory to Lessor in its sole discretion, which (i) describes all units of Equipment ordered, received and accepted by the Company as agent for Lessor in connection with such Schedule, and (ii) if Company has paid any Supplier for any of the Equipment, includes evidence of the Purchase Price paid to Supplier for each such unit of Equipment and of passage of title thereto to Lessor. Upon the latter of (A) Lessor's receipt of the Purchase Documentation or (B) the satisfaction of all conditions precedent within 60 days of the applicable Lease Commencement Date, Lessor shall pay the Supplier or reimburse the Company, as the case may be, for the aggregate Purchase Price for all Equipment purchased hereunder in connection with such schedule.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'within 60 days of the applicable Lease Commencement Date' as a condition for the Lessor to pay the Supplier or reimburse the Company. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text which states 'Upon the latter of (A) Lessor's receipt of the Purchase Documentation or (B) the satisfaction of all conditions precedent on or after the applicable Lease Commencement Date' without any time restriction. Introducing the 60-day limit creates an inconsistency regarding when the payment should be made, potentially leading to disputes if conditions precedent are satisfied after the 60-day period. Therefore, this is an instance of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract now provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the agent of Lessor, without any fee for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the agent of Lessor, with a fixed monthly fee of $5,000 for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the appointment condition where the Company is now receiving a fixed monthly fee for acting as such agent. The original text states that Lessor appoints the Company as the agent of Lessor, without any fee for acting as such agent, leading to conflicting contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the agent of Lessor, with a fixed monthly fee of $5,000 for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Company acts as an agent 'without any fee,' while the changed text specifies 'with a fixed monthly fee of $5,000.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and therefore contradicts the original agreement of no fee. This creates a direct contradiction, leading to uncertainty about whether the Company is entitled to a fee or not.
